description: Character/monster state definitions — buffs, debuffs, curses, auras, transformations, and associated visual/sound effects.

state.description: Unique state identifier used by skills.txt, missiles.txt, and other files to reference this state

id.description: Sequential numeric ID for this state, must match the row index

group.description: Group ID for mutually exclusive states — states in the same group cannot stack (e.g., cold armors share a group)

remhit.description: Whether this state is dispelled when the unit is hit (only works for monsters, not players)

nosend.description: Whether the server skips sending this state to other clients (used for internal-only states that do not affect other players)

transform.description: Whether this state transforms the unit appearance (e.g., werewolf, werebear, Holy Shield)

aura.description: Whether this is an aura state — auras cycle on-screen display and are dispelled by conversion effects

curable.description: Whether a heal-enabled NPC (e.g., Akara) can remove this state when you talk to them

curse.description: Whether this is a curse — curse states cannot stack and a new curse replaces the previous one

active.description: Whether the state has an active ongoing effect (e.g., Shiver Armor's retaliation)

immed.description: Whether the state takes effect immediately upon application without waiting for the next game tick

restrict.description: Whether the state restricts player actions (related to werewolf/werebear transformation restrictions)

disguise.description: Whether the state changes the unit graphic entirely (e.g., werewolf/werebear/valkyrie appearance swap)

blue.description: Whether the state is considered a positive (blue) modifier for UI display purposes

attblue.description: Whether to display the attack rating modifier from this state in blue (positive) color

damblue.description: Whether to display the damage modifier from this state in blue (positive) color

armblue.description: Whether to display the armor/defense modifier from this state in blue (positive) color

rfblue.description: Whether to display the fire resistance modifier from this state in blue (positive) color

rlblue.description: Whether to display the lightning resistance modifier from this state in blue (positive) color

rcblue.description: Whether to display the cold resistance modifier from this state in blue (positive) color

stambarblue.description: Whether to display the stamina bar effect from this state in blue (positive) color

rpblue.description: Whether to display the poison resistance modifier from this state in blue (positive) color

attred.description: Whether to display the attack rating modifier from this state in red (negative) color

damred.description: Whether to display the damage modifier from this state in red (negative) color

armred.description: Whether to display the armor/defense modifier from this state in red (negative) color

rfred.description: Whether to display the fire resistance modifier from this state in red (negative) color

rlred.description: Whether to display the lightning resistance modifier from this state in red (negative) color

rcred.description: Whether to display the cold resistance modifier from this state in red (negative) color

rpred.description: Whether to display the poison resistance modifier from this state in red (negative) color

exp.description: Whether this state is an expansion-only state (Lord of Destruction content)

plrstaydeath.description: Whether this state persists on a player after death (not removed on resurrection)

monstaydeath.description: Whether this state persists on a monster after death (e.g., for corpse-related effects)

bossstaydeath.description: Whether this state persists on a boss monster after death

hide.description: Whether the state is hidden from the player UI and buff/debuff display

shatter.description: Whether a unit killed while in this state shatters (ice death animation, no corpse left)

udead.description: Whether the state marks the unit as undead for purposes of holy damage and other undead-specific effects

life.description: Whether this state affects the life display or life-related behavior

green.description: Whether the state is displayed in green color in the UI (set bonus style)

pgsv.description: Whether this state is associated with progressive (charge-up) skills and triggers when charges activate

nooverlays.description: Whether to suppress overlay graphics while this state is active on the unit

noclear.description: Whether this state cannot be cleared by normal means (e.g., town portal or death)

bossinv.description: Whether the state uses the minion owner's inventory client-side (makes Decoy display your current equipment)

meleeonly.description: Whether the state restricts the unit to melee attacks only (prevents shapeshifted druids from firing missiles with bows)

notondead.description: Whether this state cannot be applied to dead units

overlay1.description: First persistent overlay displayed on the unit while this state is active

overlay2.description: Second persistent overlay displayed on the unit while this state is active

overlay3.description: Third persistent overlay displayed on the unit while this state is active

overlay4.description: Fourth persistent overlay displayed on the unit while this state is active

pgsvoverlay.description: Overlay shown on the target of a progressive skill when the charge-up triggers

castoverlay.description: Overlay displayed once when the state is initially applied (cast effect)

removerlay.description: Overlay displayed once when the state is removed

stat.description: Stat from itemstatcost.txt associated with this state (used to link state duration or strength to a stat)

setfunc.description: Server function ID called when this state is applied to a unit

remfunc.description: Server function ID called when this state is removed from a unit

missile.description: Missile spawned while this state is active (e.g., Blaze fire trail)

skill.description: Skill associated with this state (used to link back to the skill that created it)

itemtype.description: Item type restriction — limits which item types can grant this state (references ItemTypes.txt Code)

itemtrans.description: Color transform applied to items while this state is active

colorpri.description: Priority for the color transform — higher priority overrides lower when multiple states are active

colorshift.description: Palette shift index applied to the unit graphic while this state is active

light-r.description: Red component of the light radius color emitted while this state is active

light-g.description: Green component of the light radius color emitted while this state is active

light-b.description: Blue component of the light radius color emitted while this state is active

onsound.description: Sound played when this state is applied

offsound.description: Sound played when this state expires or is removed

gfxtype.description: Graphics type used for the state visual effect (determines which graphic class to use)

gfxclass.description: Graphics class index selecting the specific visual variant within the gfxtype

cltevent.description: Client event name that triggers clteventfunc (e.g., 'hit', 'kill')

clteventfunc.description: Client-side function ID executed when the cltevent fires

cltactivefunc.description: Client-side function ID called every frame while the state is active on the unit

srvactivefunc.description: Server-side function ID called every frame while the state is active on the unit

eol.description: End of line marker — always 0, used by the game to detect row termination
